title: Kebla Yurt's Equipment Emporium
content:

{{Quote|Welcome to the Equipment Emporium! You looking to buy some supplies?|Kebla Yurt|<ref name="kotor" />}}
'''Kebla Yurt's Equipment Emporium''' was a shop owned by [[Kebla Yurt]] located within the [[Upper City]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. The shop's chief products included [[Computer/Legends|computer]] [[Slicer/Legends|slicing]] equipment, [[Armor/Legends|armor]], weapons, and explosive items such as [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]] and [[permacrete detonator]]s, as well as large items such as [[Swoop/Legends|swoops]] and [[Speeder/Legends|speeders]]. The shop survived the [[Taris Siege|siege of Taris]] and the planet's subsequent occupation by the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]], and continued to do business during the years of the [[Jedi Civil War]] and the planetary [[Blockade/Legends|quarantine]] instituted by the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]]. The [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], however, confiscated the shop's heavy weapons and speeders. While trapped on Taris with [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] officer [[Carth Onasi]] following the [[Attack on the Endar Spire|destruction]] of the ''[[Endar Spire]]'', [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]—the amnesiac former [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]—visited Yurt's shop. The Equipment Emporium was open for business until [[Darth Malak]]'s [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital]] [[Destruction of Taris|bombardment]] of the planet in [[3956 BBY]].

==Location and layout==
[[Kebla Yurt]]'s Equipment Emporium was located within a simple, one-room store that was situated near the [[Upper City Cantina]], within the [[Upper City]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. The store was centered around a single counter at which Yurt, the shop's [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] proprietor, conducted business with her customers. Scattered about the shop were a wide variety of items, as well as a number of [[Footlocker/Legends|footlockers]] and other [[Plasteel/Legends|plasteel]] and metal containers. Also scattered along the walls were a number of [[Computer/Legends|computer]] terminals as well as [[Droid/Legends|droid]] repair and upgrade stations. When the store was closed, the room was sealed off via a large [[Blast door/Legends|blast door]].<ref name="kotor" />

==History==
===Founding and operations===
{{Quote|Uh, just so you know, all prices are final. This isn't a swap meet.|Kebla Yurt|<ref name="kotor" />}}
[[File:KeblaYurt-KotOR.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Kebla Yurt]], the Emporium's owner]]
The Emporium was founded prior to the [[Taris Siege|siege]] of Taris during the [[Mandalorian Wars]], and briefly served as a hiding spot for several [[Taris Holofeed]] employees when their offices at [[Highpoint]] were destroyed.<ref name="holofeed" /> It was still doing business in the Upper City in [[3956 BBY]], when the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]], under [[Darth Malak]], conquered the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], instituted a [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] that prohibited all access to and travel from Taris, and set patrols of [[Sith trooper (Sith Empire)|Sith troopers]] to police the Upper City. The reason for these actions, however, was kept from the populace: the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], at Malak's command, were searching for and attempting to capture a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Bastila Shan]], whose abilities with [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] the Dark Lord coveted, after she had escaped the [[Attack on the Endar Spire|destruction]] of her vessel, the ''[[Endar Spire]]'', above Taris. As a direct consequence, Sith forces confiscated all heavy weapons, [[Swoop/Legends|swoops]], and [[Speeder/Legends|speeders]] that the Emporium had available for sale. Nonetheless, a brisk trade in weapons, [[Armor/Legends|armor]], and computer-[[Slicer/Legends|slicing]] equipment was allowed to continue; this was, however, limited due to the fact that no new stock could be imported from off-world. In addition, prices at the Emporium were as marked, with no negotiations permitted.<ref name="kotor" />

===During the Tarisian blockade===
{{Quote|Hey! I know you! You're the Mysterious Stranger!|Kebla Yurt, to Revan|<ref name="kotor" />}}
{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic}}
Several days after the blockade was instituted,<ref>This assumes that [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] was placed under planetary quarantine soon after the ''[[Endar Spire]]'' was destroyed; as such, Carth describes Revan as having been "out" for three days.</ref> the amnesiac former [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], accompanied by [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] officer and fellow ''Endar Spire'' escapee [[Carth Onasi]], visited the Emporium. The pair discussed current events with Yurt, as they were seeking information about a number of [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]], one of which contained Shan, that had crashed into the [[Undercity (Taris)|Undercity]], the lowest level of the planet's stratified world-city. Yurt confirmed that reports and rumors about downed Republic pods were circulating, and that other parties besides the Sith were interested in finding them; these included the [[Exchange]], an interstellar crime syndicate whose operations on Taris were managed by [[Davik Kang]], and a number of [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gangs]] including the [[Hidden Beks]] and [[Black Vulkars/Legends|Black Vulkars]]. When asked about Kang and the gangs, Yurt informed the pair that conditions in the [[Lower City]] were becoming more and more dangerous due to the fighting going on between them.<ref name="kotor" />

Yurt recognized Revan as the "[[Mysterious Stranger]]," one of the contenders who fought in the [[Taris dueling ring]]. At some point, Revan encountered an ex-Exchange member and informant, a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] by the name of [[Matrik]], who was living in a run-down apartment in the Lower City. Matrik told Revan of his plight, describing how he had grown disillusioned with the Exchange, leaving them and turning state's evidence against the criminal syndicate; for this, Exchange boss Kang had a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] placed on his head. After suggesting that Matrik fake his own death, Revan was told of a way to make it happen, by acquiring a [[permacrete detonator]] that could be used to destroy the apartment and make it appear as though Matrik had died in the blast. Revan then went back to the Emporium, purchased the device, and gave it to Matrik, who set it off before disappearing. Revan later collected the bounty from [[Zax]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], who ran the Tarisian governmental bounty offices, claiming responsibility for the deed. Zax believed the ruse, stating that some of his people had witnessed the purchasing of the detonator and reported it.<ref name="kotor" />
{{Endgame}}

===Destruction===
{{Quote|The search for Bastila is taking too long. We cannot risk her escaping Taris. Destroy the entire planet.|Darth Malak, to [[Saul Karath]]|<ref name="kotor" />}}
Some time later, Darth Malak became frustrated with the fruitless search for Shan. Knowing that the more time that passed without finding her, the more likely it was that she could escape, the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] ordered his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] to conduct an [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]] of the planet, with the intent of ridding [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] of Shan if he could not have her and her abilities for himself.<ref name="kotor" /> During the fusillade of [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] fire that followed, the surface of Taris [[Destruction of Taris|was devastated]], with few buildings left standing.<ref name="boom" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kebla Yurt's Equipment Emporium was first introduced in the [[Wikipedia:Personal computer|computer]] and [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', developed by [[BioWare]]. The company also received a brief mention in the [[in-universe]] news supplement ''[[The Taris Holofeed: Invasion Edition]]'', which was printed in [[Knights of the Old Republic 18|''Nights of Anger, Part 3'']], the eighteenth issue of the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Knights of the Old Republic'']] series of [[Star Wars comic books|comic books]]. The shop later gained an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', where it was first identified as Kebla Yurt's Equipment Emporium.

The player, as Revan, is not required to enter the Equipment Emporium or interact with its proprietor, Kebla Yurt, at all, as it is not the only general-item retailer within the Taris Upper City levels of the game. However, in most instances it is convenient to do so, particularly for the information Yurt gives to an uninitiated player, the store's proximity to other areas, specifically the Upper City Cantina, and the fact that the player can sell the items that they find throughout the planet for any [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] that might be needed. The [[canon]]icity of Revan's taking on Bendak Starkiller, which earns the player [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark-side]] points, is uncertain due to the canon ending of the game being the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light-side]] choice.<ref name="Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force real-life book">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> However, this article assumes total game completion, which involves the purchase of the permacrete detonator for Matrik.<ref name="guide">[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref>
